lede: A look at June 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jackie Stewart and Claire Holt, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2012 Afghanistan earthquakes
events.desc: 75 people die in a landslide triggered by two earthquakes in Afghanistan; an entire village is buried.

events.title: 2007 Chittagong mudslides
events.desc: Mudslides in Chittagong, Bangladesh, kill 130 people.

events.title: Timothy McVeigh
events.desc: Timothy McVeigh is executed for his role in the Oklahoma City bombing.

events.title: June 1962 Alcatraz escape
events.desc: Frank Morris, John Anglin and Clarence Anglin allegedly become the only prisoners to escape from the prison on Alcatraz Island.

events.title: 1920 Republican National Convention
events.desc: During the U.S. Republican National Convention in Chicago, U.S. Republican Party leaders gathered in a room at the Blackstone Hotel to come to a consensus on their candidate for the U.S. presidential election, leading the Associated Press to coin the political phrase "smoke-filled room".

events.title: Alexander of Greece
events.desc: King Alexander assumes the throne of Greece after his father, Constantine I, is deemed to have abdicated under pressure from allied armies occupying Athens.

events.title: Denmark
events.desc: Denmark adopts the characteristic Nordic Cross flag later taken up by all other Scandinavian countries.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Johann Sebastian Bach leads his cantata O Ewigkeit, du Donnerwort (O eternity, you word of thunder), BWV 20, on the first Sunday after Trinity, beginning his second cycle, the chorale cantata cycle.

events.title: Philip II of Spain
events.desc: Philip II recognizes the rights and privileges of the local nobles and chieftains in the Philippines, which paved way to the stabilization of the rule of the Principalía (an elite ruling class of native nobility in Spanish Philippines).

events.title: Megas doux
events.desc: The megas doux Alexios Apokaukos, chief minister of the Byzantine Empire, is lynched by political prisoners.

births.name: Jackie Stewart
births.line: Scottish racing driver and sports presenter

births.name: Yasunari Kawabata
births.line: Japanese novelist and short story writer Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: John Wayne, American actor, director, and producer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bartholomew the Apostle (Eastern Christianity)
Christian feast day: Blessed Ignatius Maloyan (Armenian Catholic Church)
